input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-04-04T00:55:39.638Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
Here's how to make your page theme-aware:

## XAML ‚Äî `AppThemeBinding`

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome!"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page follows the system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

`AppThemeBinding` reacts automatically when the OS theme changes ‚Äî no event wiring needed.

## C# alternative ‚Äî `SetAppTheme`

The equivalent in code-behind or a programmatic page:

```csharp
// Extension method on any BindableObject
myPage.SetAppTheme(
    ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Colors.White,   // Light
    Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E")); // Dark

titleLabel.SetAppTheme(
    Label.TextColorProperty,
    Colors.Black,
    Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));

actionButton.SetAppTheme(
    Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"),
    Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));
```

## Key points

| Concern | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | Follows `Application.RequestedTheme` (`Light` / `Dark` / `Unspecified`) |
| **Live updates** | Both approaches update instantly when the user toggles the system theme |
| **Resources** | You can also use `AppThemeBinding` with `StaticResource` / `DynamicResource` to reference theme-specific resource dictionaries |
| **Override** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme` to force a specific theme app-wide |

For larger apps, define colors in a `ResourceDictionary` and reference them, so you only maintain values in one place:

```xml
<!-- Resources/Styles/Colors.xaml -->
<Color x:Key="PageBg">{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}</Color>

<!-- Usage -->
<ContentPage BackgroundColor="{StaticResource PageBg}" />
```
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for guidance on how to use `AppThemeBinding` in .NET MAUI to make a ContentPage theme-aware. This is a knowledge/explanation question, not a code change task on an existing repo. I'll provide a concise, practical example.
